questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: According to one report, the cases of kidnapping have been on the rise exponentially over the last couple of years. Courses of action: I. Stricter laws should be enacted against kidnapping. II. A separate monitoring cell should be constituted in the police department to monitor cases of kidnapping. III. Children should be taught self-defence skills as well so as judo to keep kidnappers away.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: In a calamity, over 62 persons were frozen to death in Kashmir as a result of avalanches and landslides. Courses of action: I. The government should give compensations to the families of the deceased. II. The government should evacuate the affected areas immediately. III. The government should come down heavily on terrorists.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: The National Human Rights Commission has been set up for the purpose of evaluating the human rights scenario of the country and ensuring that gross violations of human rights do not occur. Courses of action: I. The Government should conduct elections to the NHRC for making it a democratic body. II. The Government should extend all possible help to NHRC for smooth functioning. III. The NHRC should be given adequate powers to be able to handle the cases of human rights violations.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: It is suspected that one of the main reasons behind the Jamma bomb carnage on this Republic Day could be the complaint of some government officials. Courses of action: I. The Government should ask all government officials to go on leave until the truth is finally out. II. The Government should set up an enquiry commission to look into the matter. III. Security arrangements should be tightened in and around the area.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: Crores of rupees are spent each year for the purpose of removing illiteracy, but the plan has failed to deliver. Courses of action: I. The spending should be permanently abandoned. II. The spending should be temporarily halted. III. The government should employ funds in some more rewarding areas.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: The film industry has been on the downslide because of the production of unauthorised videos. Courses of action: I. Persons making unauthorised videos should be sentenced to life imprisonment. II. The film industry should make commercially lucrative films. III. The government should waive entertainment tax levied upon such films.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: As a result of communal tensions in the city, everyday activity has come to a halt. Courses of action: I. The government should employ additional security. II. Care should be taken to stop rumours from spreading. III. Extremist religious leaders of both the communities should be talked.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: According to one survey, noise pollution in the metros is likely to make at least ten thousand people deaf in the next five years. Courses of action: I. Noise pollution should be declared as the most sensitive issue by the ministry of health. II. Fines should be imposed on vehicles, factories etc. that emit sound at a higher level than permitted. III. People should be made aware of this prospect.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: Because of heavy rains for the last three days, the water level of the river has crossed the danger mark.
